subject: ELA
title: Reading: The Amazon Rainforest
passage:
The Amazon Rainforest

The Amazon rainforest is the world's largest tropical rainforest. It covers most of the Amazon basin in South America and spans across nine countries. The Amazon is often called the 'lungs of the Earth' because its plants produce about 20% of the world's oxygen.

The rainforest is home to an incredible variety of wildlife. Scientists have identified more than 40,000 plant species, 1,300 bird species, and 3,000 types of fish. Many animals found in the Amazon live nowhere else on Earth, including the pink river dolphin and the poison dart frog.

The forest also faces serious threats. Deforestation — the cutting down of trees — has destroyed millions of acres of forest. Farmers clear land for cattle and crops. Mining and logging have also caused damage. Scientists warn that if deforestation continues, many species could become extinct.

Protecting the Amazon is important for the whole planet. Many countries are working together to reduce deforestation and help the forest recover.

questions.q: Why is the Amazon called the 'lungs of the Earth'?
options: Its plants produce ~20% of world oxygen
questions.answer: Its plants produce ~20% of world oxygen
questions.explanation: The passage directly states plants produce about 20% of the world's oxygen.

questions.q: Which animal is UNIQUE to the Amazon?
questions.explanation: The pink river dolphin lives nowhere else on Earth.

questions.q: How many bird species have been identified in the Amazon?
questions.explanation: 1,300 bird species, as stated in the passage.

questions.q: What does 'deforestation' mean according to the passage?
questions.explanation: The passage defines it: 'the cutting down of trees.'

questions.q: Which detail supports the idea that the Amazon faces threats?
questions.explanation: Mining causing damage is a specific threat named in the passage.

questions.q: What is the MAIN IDEA of this passage?
options: The Amazon's importance and the threats it faces
questions.answer: The Amazon's importance and the threats it faces
questions.explanation: The passage covers what the Amazon is, why it matters, and threats to it.

questions.q: How many countries does the Amazon rainforest span?
What inference can you make from the last paragraph?
options: People believe the Amazon can still be saved
questions.answer: People believe the Amazon can still be saved
questions.explanation: Countries working together to reduce deforestation implies hope.

questions.q: What would be a good TITLE for the third paragraph?
questions.explanation: The third paragraph is entirely about threats: deforestation, farming, mining, logging.

questions.q: Which word BEST describes the tone of this passage?
questions.explanation: The author informs readers while expressing concern about the Amazon's threats.
